query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "full attention strikes back: transferring full attention into sparse w" linguistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it investigates the same core mechanism (sparse attention) and domain (long-context language models) referenced in the user's question, specifically addressing the trade-offs between efficiency and stability that are central to the "Full Attention Strikes Back" follow-up context. Although the user's query mentions "linguistics," the paper provides the essential empirical baseline and architectural evidence for the "sparse vs. full attention" mechanism
